danger_signals.visual_form: Radar pulse visualization on HUD; visual comparison of player radar signature against incoming energy pulses at varying ranges and powers
danger_signals.meaning: Player aircraft has been detected by enemy SAM sites or radar stations; immediate threat of engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: AI-controlled SAM behavior shift from patrol patterns to aggressive search mode
danger_signals.meaning: Air defense system transitioning from passive monitoring to active threat posture

danger_signals.visual_form: AI-controlled enemy aircraft behavior shift to highly aggressive attack patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate air combat threat; player is engaged by hostile fighter

danger_signals.visual_form: Detection state during Cold War difficulty mode
danger_signals.meaning: In Cold War conditions, mere detection by enemy forces can trigger international diplomatic crisis

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of radar pulses on HUD; stealth signature below enemy detection threshold
safety_signals.meaning: Player maintains covert approach; enemy units remain in patrol mode

safety_signals.visual_form: Low radar signature visualization relative to incoming radar energy
safety_signals.meaning: Aircraft design and tactics successfully minimizing detection risk

safety_signals.visual_form: SAM sites and radar stations displaying predictable patrol patterns rather than search sweeps
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy forces unaware of player presence; no active threat posture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: F-19/F-117 stealth aircraft; U.S. Air Force designation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled asset; represents U.S. military technological superiority in stealth aviation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: SAM sites, radar stations, enemy aircraft
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: AI-controlled hostile units with realistic Soviet-era air defense systems
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Referenced in manual documentation of Soviet aircraft; opposing force in all mission scenarios

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Geographic mission locations represent different political entities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mission locations: Gaddafi's Libya, Persian Gulf, North Cape, Central Europe
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Environmental storytelling through mission briefing locations and geopolitical context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard mission parameters; full combat engagement rules apply
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI units behave according to military doctrine; SAM sites and radar stations patrol and respond to detection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Restricted engagement rules; selected targets only
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy units maintain operational posture but engagement is constrained by rules of engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Detection itself becomes critical threat indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Being spotted by any enemy force triggers diplomatic incident; emphasis on stealth over combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescue sequence visualization; SH-3 Sea King helicopter arrival and extraction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player successfully evades detection and completes mission objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pravda newspaper headline announcing pilot capture; text-based narrative outcome
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player detected and neutralized; geopolitical consequences displayed through Soviet media representation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protest from allied or neutral nation; text-based diplomatic consequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unintended target destruction or detection escalates international tension

environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct geographic theaters communicate Cold War tensions: Gaddafi's Libya (regional proxy conflict), Persian Gulf (resource competition), North Cape (Arctic strategic interest), Central Europe (NATO-Warsaw Pact confrontation)
Pravda newspaper headline as failure outcome communicates Soviet perspective and propaganda use of downed pilot; signals geopolitical consequences beyond individual mission
Allied or neutral nation outrage at aircraft destruction communicates international law violation and collateral damage consequences within Cold War rules of engagement
Permanent pilot fatalities in roster system communicate stakes and campaign consequence; visual tracking of missions, rank, medals creates narrative continuity of ongoing conflict
Comprehensive manual with U.S. and Soviet aircraft specifications communicates technological rivalry and contextualizes aircraft selection within real Cold War equipment inventories
Game release synchronized with U.S. government public acknowledgment of F-117A; stealth technology as the visual/mechanical embodiment of technological secret-keeping and strategic surprise

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol pattern; passive scanning; no active target lock
Shift to aggressive search mode; active radar engagement; targeting behavior
Routine monitoring; standard radar sweep pattern
Increased scan intensity; communication with air defense and combat units; elevated alert posture
Predictable flight patterns; standard patrol routes
Highly aggressive attack patterns; pursuit and intercept tactics; sustained engagement
Radar signature below detection threshold; can approach targets undetected
Immediate escalation to evasion and combat; reduced tactical options; increased danger

notes: F-19 Stealth Fighter is a Cold War-era air combat simulation that communicates world-state primarily through detection mechanics and geopolitical mission framing. The core visual communication system centers on radar signature visualization—the player's radar presence relative to incoming radar pulses creates the primary danger indicator. World-state is communicated through three difficulty modes (Conventional/Limited/Cold War) that fundamentally alter the consequence structure: in Cold War mode, detection itself becomes failure. Mission outcomes—rescue, capture, diplomatic protest—provide text-based and behavioral consequences that reinforce geopolitical stakes. The game's release timing (same day as U.S. government acknowledgment of F-117A) and inclusion of both fictional F-19 and real F-117 variants communicates the historical moment of stealth technology transition from secret to public. Environmental storytelling occurs through geographic mission selection (Libya, Persian Gulf, North Cape, Central Europe), each representing distinct Cold War flashpoint regions. Faction identity is implicit rather than visually distinctive: the player embodies U.S. technological superiority through stealth capability, while Soviet air defense systems represent the opposing technological and ideological system. The pilot roster permanence system creates long-term narrative consequence that extends individual mission outcomes into campaign-level stakes.
